intro:
Scruff is a niche dating app for gay, bi, and queer men with a strong bear community focus. It offers solid social features but stays closed to other identities and requires Scruff Pro for most advanced features.

Embir offers an alternative for gay and bi men wanting a platform with verified profiles, reporting and blocking, and openness to all orientations.

comparisonTable.embir: Core features core connection features are free
Fully inclusive for trans and non-binary

sections.title: Beyond the bear niche — a broader platform
sections.content: Scruff has a solidly built community around the bear niche and gay travelers. If that's your identity, Scruff does it well. If you want a gay platform with a broader base — including bi, trans, non-binary users — Embir covers that territory with verified profiles.
highlights: All LGBTQ+ identities included — not just cis men
Selfie verification vs Scruff's basic photo check
free for core connections vs Scruff Pro subscription

verdict: Scruff excels for the bear gay community and traveling gay men. Embir is a better alternative if you want a broader gay platform with verified profiles, trans/NB inclusion, and free access.
ctaText: Inclusive gay dating, verified — join Embir

faq.q: Is Embir good for gay and bi men?
faq.a: Yes. Bidirectional filtering ensures you only see profiles matching your orientation and vice versa. Verified profiles and reporting and blocking.

faq.q: Does Embir include trans men and non-binary people?
faq.a: Yes. Embir supports all gender identities in its matching system.

faq.q: Is Embir free unlike Scruff Pro?
faq.a: Yes. Core connection features are free for everyone. No Pro subscription required.
